The Tenebrix feeds on bacteria, fungi, and other microorganisms that thrive in the absence of light. Its specialized fibers can extend and contract to capture and engulf prey, while its dark-sensitive nodules help to detect the presence of nutrients. The Tenebrix can also secrete enzymes to break down complex organic matter, allowing it to feed on a wide range of substances.
